key_insight: This survey shows that magneto-active polymers at finite strains are best captured by a family of nonlinear, thermodynamically consistent constitutive models that account for anisotropy, field-induced stiffening, and dissipative effects.
engineering_relevance: The reviewed models provide the theoretical basis for designing and simulating MAP-based sensors, actuators, vibration-control devices, adaptive structures, and soft robotic components.
method: Reviews isotropic and anisotropic magnetoelastic constitutive laws
Covers invariant-based, spectral, variational, and polyconvex formulations
Includes microstructure-driven and magneto-viscoelastic modeling approaches
evidence_signal: The paper is a structured literature survey synthesizing model classes, physical assumptions, coupling mechanisms, and reported experimental behaviors rather than presenting a new dataset.
watch_item: A key limitation is that reliable use in practice still depends on solving thermo-magneto-mechanical coupling, parameter identification, and robust numerical implementation.
paper_title: Constitutive modelling of magneto-active polymers at finite strains: A survey
